name: Gerald "Gerry" Kowalski
chief_complaint: I've been throwing up blood since last night, looks like coffee grounds, and my stools are black and tarry
onset_description: Started vomiting dark material resembling coffee grounds approximately 10 hours ago. Noticed black, foul-smelling stools this morning. Has had worsening epigastric pain for the past 2 weeks. Feels lightheaded when standing.

hematemesis.description: Threw up three times, looked like dark coffee grounds mixed in with it
hematemesis.character: dark brown/black emesis with granular appearance

melena.description: My stool is black and sticky like tar, smells awful

epigastric_pain.description: Burning pain right here in the pit of my stomach, been going on a couple weeks but got way worse yesterday

dizziness.description: I get real woozy when I stand up, almost blacked out in the bathroom

weakness.description: I feel wiped out, no energy at all, legs feel like jelly

labs.BMP: Na 137, K 3.4 (low-normal), Cr 1.3 (mildly elevated), Glucose 98, BUN 42 (elevated - suggests upper GI bleed)
labs.liver_function: AST 68 (elevated), ALT 52 (elevated), Alk Phos 95, Total Bilirubin 1.4, Albumin 3.0 (low)

past_medical_history: History of peptic ulcer disease (diagnosed 3 years ago, non-compliant with treatment)
medications: Ibuprofen 600mg three times daily (self-medicating for chronic knee pain)
Lisinopril 10mg daily (admits to missing doses frequently)
Omeprazole 20mg daily (stopped taking it 2 months ago because he 'felt fine')

social_history.alcohol: 6-8 beers daily, more on weekends. Drinks whiskey too. Last drink was yesterday evening.
social_history.living_situation: Divorced, lives alone in a house. Ex-wife brought him to the ED.
social_history.diet: Irregular meals, lots of fried food, eats out most meals

personality: gruff and irritable, defensive about his drinking, impatient with the process but underneath is scared
communication_style: Blunt and sometimes rude. Minimizes his alcohol use when first asked. Gets defensive if pressed about lifestyle. Uses profanity casually. Will eventually open up if treated with respect and without judgment. Asks 'how long is this gonna take?' frequently.
pain_description_style: Understates pain. Describes it in functional terms like 'it ain't nothing I can't handle' but then grimaces. Uses working-class analogies: 'feels like battery acid in my gut.'

critical_findings: Active upper GI hemorrhage with hematemesis and melena
Significant anemia (Hgb 8.2) suggesting substantial blood loss
Elevated BUN:Cr ratio consistent with upper GI bleed
High-risk NSAID use (ibuprofen 600mg TID) with known PUD history
Heavy alcohol use with elevated liver enzymes and positive ethanol level
Mildly prolonged INR suggesting hepatic synthetic dysfunction
rubric.diagnosis: Upper GI Bleed - likely peptic ulcer (NSAID and alcohol-related)
recommended_interventions: Two large-bore IV access (18 gauge or larger)
Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation with crystalloids
Type and crossmatch for 4 units pRBCs
Transfuse pRBCs for Hgb < 7 or hemodynamic instability
Urgent GI consult for emergent EGD
Foley catheter for urine output monitoring
